steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the text. The passage has two clear parts: an opening that explains how cats and toads have 3D vision by combining two eye images, followed by a description of a study that investigates 3D vision in praying mantises and finds it works differently.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The text explains the general differences between the vision of cats and toads, then introduces a study of praying mantises' vision.': The passage does not compare cats and toads to each other or explain differences between them — it groups them together as animals that share the same 3D vision mechanism. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The text discusses 3D vision in cats and toads, then introduces a study about this kind of vision in praying mantises.': Accurately reflects both parts: the passage covers 3D vision in cats and toads, then turns to the mantis study. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The text reviews the similarities in how cats and toads see, then provides details of a study about praying mantises to confirm these similarities.': The study does not confirm similarities — it concludes that mantis 3D vision is "unlike that of other animals." The direction of the finding is wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The text describes how cats and toads see, then presents a study about praying mantises that challenges the accuracy of that description.': The mantis study does not challenge how cats and toads see; it simply finds that mantises see differently. The study accepts the earlier description as accurate. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
